issues.title: RFI page missing Ball In Court tracking and overdue detection per acceptance criteria
issues.description: PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA requires Ball In Court (BIC) color-coded indicator, overdue detection with red badge when due_date < today AND status !== closed, days open calculation, and KPI bar showing total open/overdue/avg days to close. None of these exist. The page likely shows a basic table without these critical construction workflow indicators.
issues.prompt: In the file src/pages/RFIs.tsx, make these specific changes: Add a KPI stats bar above the table with four MetricBox cards: Total Open (count where status !== 'closed'), Overdue (count where due_date < today AND status !== 'closed', shown with red color), Avg Days to Close (mean of closed_at - created_at for closed RFIs), and Closed This Week (count closed in last 7 days). In the table, add a 'Days Open' column calculated as Math.floor((Date.now() - new Date(rfi.created_at).getTime()) / 86400000) for open RFIs or Math.floor((new Date(rfi.closed_at).getTime() - new Date(rfi.created_at).getTime()) / 86400000) for closed ones. Add a 'Ball In Court' column showing the assigned_to name with a colored dot: blue (#3B82F6) for GC, purple (#8B5CF6) for Architect, orange (#F47820) for Owner. Add overdue row highlighting: if due_date exists and new Date(due_date) < new Date() and status !== 'closed', show a red 'OVERDUE' Tag badge next to the due date and tint the row background with rgba(231,76,60,0.05). BEFORE: Basic table with no BIC, no overdue detection, no days open, no KPI bar. AFTER: Full RFI register with BIC tracking, overdue badges, days open column, and 4 KPI metric cards matching PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA section 3. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: Submittals page missing approval chain visualization and spec section tracking
issues.description: PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA section 4 requires: approval chain as horizontal stepper showing each reviewer with decision status, spec section column linked to CSI MasterFormat, revision number tracking, lead time calculation showing days from approval to material delivery, and 'Approved as Noted' status with markup attachment. The current page lacks these construction-specific submittal workflow elements.
issues.prompt: In the file src/pages/Submittals.tsx, make these specific changes: Add a 'Spec Section' column to the table displaying the specification_section field (e.g. '03 30 00'). Add a 'Rev #' column showing revision count from submittal_revisions if available, defaulting to 0. Add a 'Current Reviewer' column showing the assigned_to field with a status indicator. Add status filter tabs above the table: All, Pending, Under Review, Approved, Rejected, Resubmit. Map status values to colored Tags: pending=#F5A623 amber, approved=#4EC896 green, rejected=#E74C3C red, approved_as_noted=#3B82F6 blue, resubmit=#8B5CF6 purple. Add KPI metric cards at top: Total Submittals, Pending Review (amber), Approved (green), Overdue (red, where due_date < today and status not in approved/approved_as_noted). BEFORE: Basic submittal list without spec sections, revision tracking, reviewer chain, or proper status pipeline visualization. AFTER: Professional submittal register with CSI spec sections, revision numbers, reviewer indicators, status-colored tags, filter tabs, and KPI cards per PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA section 4. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: Change Orders page missing 3-tier PCO/COR/CO pipeline and cost breakdown
issues.description: PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA section 15 requires: CO numbers sequential and immutable, cost breakdown by type (labor/material/equipment/sub with markup), schedule impact field, status workflow Draft→Pending→Approved/Rejected, running total of approved COs showing net contract change, and reason categorization (owner-directed, field-condition, design-error, value-engineering). The page lacks these financial workflow elements critical for GC budget control.
issues.prompt: In the file src/pages/ChangeOrders.tsx, make these specific changes: Add a summary bar at the top with 5 MetricBox cards: Total COs (count), Approved Total (sum of cost_impact where status=approved, formatted as currency), Pending Total (sum where status=pending), Rejected Total (sum where status=rejected), Net Contract Change (approved total formatted with +/- sign). Add a 'Reason' column to the table showing the reason field with an icon: owner_request=User icon, field_condition=AlertTriangle, design_error=FileX, value_engineering=TrendingDown. Add a 'Cost Impact' column formatted as currency using Intl.NumberFormat('en-US', {style:'currency',currency:'USD'}). Show positive amounts in red with + prefix and negative in green. Add a 'Schedule Impact' column showing schedule_impact_days with 'days' suffix, colored red if positive (delay) or green if negative (acceleration). Add status Tags: draft=#6B7280 gray, pending=#F5A623 amber, approved=#4EC896 green, rejected=#E74C3C red. BEFORE: Basic change order list without financial summary, cost formatting, reason categorization, or schedule impact display. AFTER: Full change order pipeline with financial summary bar, currency-formatted costs, reason icons, schedule impact indicators, and status workflow per PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA section 15. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: Punch List page missing two-step verification workflow and before/after photo comparison
issues.description: PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA section 8 requires: two-step verification (Sub marks Complete → GC verifies or rejects), status pipeline Open→In Progress→Sub Complete→Verified/Rejected, before/after photo display, responsible party color coding, due date aging with color thresholds (>4 days green, 1-4 amber, overdue red), and location hierarchy. None of these exist.
issues.prompt: In the file src/pages/PunchList.tsx, make these specific changes: Add KPI stats bar with 6 MetricBox cards: Total Items, Open (amber), In Progress (blue), Awaiting Verification (purple, where status='resolved' or equivalent sub-complete state), Verified (green), Overdue (red, due_date < today and status not verified/completed). In the table, add due date aging: calculate days remaining as Math.floor((new Date(item.due_date).getTime() - Date.now()) / 86400000). Color the due date text: green if >4 days, amber (#F5A623) if 1-4, red (#E74C3C) if <=0 with 'X days overdue' label. Add responsible party column with colored background: if trade includes 'electric' or 'plumb' or 'hvac' use warm background rgba(244,120,32,0.08), for GC items use rgba(59,130,246,0.08). Add status filter tabs: All, Open, In Progress, Awaiting Verification, Verified, Overdue. Map status to colored dots: open=#F5A623, in_progress=#3B82F6, resolved=#8B5CF6 (sub complete), verified=#4EC896, rejected=#E74C3C. BEFORE: Basic punch list without verification workflow, due date aging, responsible party coding, or status pipeline. AFTER: Construction-grade punch list with two-step verification indicators, aging colors, party coding, and status pipeline per PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA section 8. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: All core workflow pages lack contextual empty states with construction-specific guidance
issues.description: PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA Global Standards require every page to have a designed empty state with contextual illustration/icon, single-sentence explanation, and ONE primary CTA. Current pages show generic 'No data' or blank tables. Empty states must differ between true-empty ('No RFIs yet, create your first') and filtered-empty ('No results match your filters' with Clear Filters action). This applies to RFIs, Submittals, Change Orders, and Punch List.
issues.prompt: In the file src/pages/RFIs.tsx, make these specific changes: After the data loading logic, add an empty state check. If data array is empty and no filters are active, render a centered div with: a FileQuestion icon from lucide-react at 48px in #9CA3AF color, a heading 'No RFIs Yet' in 18px 600 weight #1A1A2E, a paragraph 'When questions arise in the field about drawings or specs, create an RFI to get a documented answer from the design team.' in 14px #6B7280, and a primary Btn component with text 'Create First RFI' using onClick to open the create modal. If data is empty but filters ARE active, show a different state: FilterX icon, heading 'No RFIs Match Your Filters', paragraph 'Try adjusting your search or filter criteria.', and a secondary button 'Clear All Filters' that resets filter state. Wrap both in a div with style={{ display:'flex', flexDirection:'column', alignItems:'center', justifyContent:'center', padding:'80px 24px', gap:'16px', textAlign:'center' }}. BEFORE: Empty table or generic 'No data found' when no RFIs exist. AFTER: Contextual empty state with construction-specific guidance, icon, and CTA differentiating true-empty from filtered-empty per PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA Global Standards. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

competitive_intel: Procore's RFI module has robust Ball In Court tracking with email reply capability where architects respond without logging in. Their change order module links directly from RFIs with cost impact auto-calculation. Fieldwire has a superior punch list with two-step photo verification and drawing pin locations. SiteSync's core workflow pages currently lack all of these foundational features, making them unusable for real construction PM workflows. The gap is not polish but missing core functionality that GCs use hourly.
summary: The Core Workflows module scores 25/100, a regression from 35 last cycle. The four key pages (RFIs, Submittals, Change Orders, Punch List) are missing fundamental construction workflow features defined in PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA: Ball In Court tracking on RFIs, approval chain visualization on Submittals, cost breakdown and 3-tier pipeline on Change Orders, and two-step verification on Punch List. All pages lack proper KPI metric bars, status-colored indicators, overdue detection, and contextual empty states. The API layer in src/api/endpoints/ has solid TypeScript patterns with proper mapping functions, but the page components do not consume the data with construction domain intelligence. No AI integration exists for RFI auto-drafting or conflict detection. The five prompts target the highest-impact domain depth and interaction quality gaps that would make these pages functional for a real GC managing a $50M project.
